skill.name: charset-fix
skill.description: Helps AI agents fix Chinese and Unicode output issues on Windows POSIX shells by applying UTF-8 settings for Python, PowerShell, and cmd.exe output.
skill.summary: Fix Windows Chinese and Unicode terminal output for AI coding agents.
skill.author: gkd2323c

security_audit.summary: Static analysis found many command-execution patterns, but most are documentation examples for encoding fixes. The helper script does run hardcoded PowerShell and cmd.exe commands, so publication should include a command-execution warning. No prompt injection, credential access, exfiltration, or malicious network behavior was found.

medium_findings.title: Hardcoded Windows Shell Commands in Helper Script
medium_findings.description: The helper script invokes PowerShell and cmd.exe through subprocess.run. The commands are hardcoded for local encoding diagnostics, but they still execute native Windows shells when a user runs the script.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: Direct subprocess.run calls execute PowerShell and cmd.exe. The risk is limited because command arguments are static and no user input is interpolated.

low_findings.title: Documentation Command Examples Misclassified as Runtime Execution
low_findings.description: Many README.md and SKILL.md findings are code blocks that teach users how to set UTF-8 output. They are not executed automatically by the skill.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The cited lines are fenced examples or explanatory tables. They do not create an automatic execution path.

low_findings.title: Hardcoded URLs Are Documentation Links
low_findings.description: The network findings point to badge links and an installation command in README.md. No source file performs HTTP requests or sends data to those URLs.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The URLs appear only in Markdown badges and clone instructions. I found no runtime network client or exfiltration behavior.

low_findings.title: Weak Cryptography and Obfuscation Alerts Are Not Supported by Context
low_findings.description: The weak cryptography hits align with encoding text, and the README entropy alert is explained by multilingual content. No cryptographic operation or obfuscated payload was found.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The inspected lines describe character encodings and Markdown metadata. There is no encrypted blob, decoder, or weak hash usage.

dangerous_patterns.title: Hardcoded Windows Shell Commands in Helper Script
dangerous_patterns.description: The helper script invokes PowerShell and cmd.exe through subprocess.run. The commands are hardcoded for local encoding diagnostics, but they still execute native Windows shells when a user runs the script.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: Direct subprocess.run calls execute PowerShell and cmd.exe. The risk is limited because command arguments are static and no user input is interpolated.

content.value_statement: Windows POSIX shells can show garbled Chinese and Unicode output from native tools. This skill gives Claude, Codex, and Claude Code practical fixes for Python, PowerShell, and cmd.exe.
actual_capabilities: Explains why Windows-native tools output GBK or CP936 text in UTF-8 POSIX shells.
Shows how to set PYTHONIOENCODING for Python command output.
Provides a PowerShell OutputEncoding pattern for Windows PowerShell.
Demonstrates a Python subprocess bridge for cmd.exe output decoded as GBK.
Includes a helper script that checks local encoding behavior and runs test output.
Documents compatibility for Git Bash, BusyBox, MSYS2, WSL, and PowerShell Core.
limitations: It targets Windows environments that use POSIX-style shells.
It does not fix terminal fonts, locale packs, or application-specific encoding bugs.
The helper script runs PowerShell and cmd.exe, so users should review it before execution.
PowerShell Core, macOS, and Linux usually do not need these fixes.

use_cases.title: Repair Agent Output in Git Bash
use_cases.description: Apply UTF-8 settings when an AI coding agent prints garbled Chinese text in Git Bash.
use_cases.target_user: Windows developers using Claude Code or Codex CLI

use_cases.title: Debug Encoding Across Native Tools
use_cases.description: Compare Python, PowerShell, and cmd.exe output paths to find where encoding changes are needed.

use_cases.title: Document Team Terminal Setup
use_cases.description: Use the examples as a shared reference for consistent Unicode output in Windows POSIX shells.
use_cases.target_user: Team leads maintaining AI agent setup guides

prompt_templates.title: Basic Encoding Check
prompt_templates.prompt: Check why Chinese output is garbled in my Windows Git Bash terminal and suggest the safest first fix.

prompt_templates.title: Python Output Fix
prompt_templates.prompt: Help me apply the charset-fix Python guidance so my agent commands print Unicode text correctly on Windows.

prompt_templates.title: PowerShell and cmd.exe Review
prompt_templates.prompt: Review my Windows terminal setup and explain which charset-fix approach applies to PowerShell and cmd.exe output.

prompt_templates.title: Team Setup Procedure
prompt_templates.prompt: Create a concise setup checklist for developers using Codex or Claude Code with Git Bash on Chinese Windows systems.

output_examples.input: A user reports garbled Chinese text from Python in Git Bash.
output_examples.output: The assistant identifies a Windows code page mismatch and recommends setting Python output to UTF-8 for the session.

output_examples.input: A user sees readable shell echo output but broken PowerShell output.
output_examples.output: The assistant explains that Windows PowerShell needs its console output encoding set before printing Unicode text.

output_examples.input: A team wants repeatable setup guidance for AI coding agents on Windows.
output_examples.output: The assistant provides a short checklist covering Python, PowerShell, cmd.exe, and when the fix is unnecessary.

best_practices: Review the helper script before running it because it invokes native Windows shells.
Start with session-level environment changes before changing global terminal settings.
Verify output with simple Unicode text before using longer automation commands.
anti_patterns: Do not run shell commands from an untrusted prompt without reviewing the exact command.
Do not apply GBK decoding on systems that already use UTF-8 output.
Do not treat terminal font problems as encoding problems without testing both cases.

faq.question: What problem does this skill solve?
faq.answer: It helps fix garbled Chinese and Unicode output when Windows-native tools write to POSIX-style terminals.

faq.question: Does it run automatically?
faq.answer: The skill provides guidance and examples. The helper script only runs when a user executes it.

faq.question: Why is the security risk medium?
faq.answer: The helper script invokes PowerShell and cmd.exe. The commands are hardcoded, but users should still review them.

faq.question: Does it send data over the network?
faq.answer: No runtime network behavior was found. README links are badges and installation references.

faq.question: Does it help on macOS or Linux?
faq.answer: Usually no. The documented issue is specific to Windows-native tools and POSIX-style shells.

faq.question: Which AI tools can use it?
faq.answer: The metadata lists Claude, Codex, and Claude Code support.
